title: Charlie Goes America All Over Everybody's Ass

jokes.text: Failure implies that she actually tried to be an actor.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Dee being a quitter at acting
jokes.punchline: Dennis argues she can't even be called a failure because that would require trying
jokes.explanation: Sharp, cutting insult with perfect Dennis logic - he's so cruel he won't even give Dee credit for failure

jokes.text: It's not your fault, sweetie. You're just not pretty enough.
jokes.setup: Dee defending her acting attempt
jokes.punchline: Frank delivers crushing assessment with paternal sweetness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank - blunt cruelty delivered with fake affection

jokes.text: Oh, thank you! That's my dad, everybody!
jokes.setup: Frank's brutal assessment of Dee's looks
jokes.punchline: Dee's forced enthusiasm introducing her cruel father
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up beat showing Dee's desperate denial

jokes.text: That's why we call it 'Charlie work.'
jokes.setup: Charlie complaining about killing rats being the worst job
jokes.punchline: Revealing they've named all terrible jobs after Charlie
jokes.explanation: Excellent world-building joke that establishes Charlie's place in the hierarchy

jokes.text: You went to Vietnam in 1993 to open up a sweatshop!
jokes.setup: Frank claiming he went to Vietnam and good men died
jokes.punchline: Reveals Frank's 'Vietnam service' was just business exploitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expected Vietnam service with Frank's capitalist exploitation

jokes.text: And a lot of good men died in that sweatshop!
jokes.setup: Charlie exposing Frank's fake Vietnam service
jokes.punchline: Frank doubling down by treating sweatshop deaths as war casualties
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Frank's complete moral blindness

jokes.text: You're practically a Vietcong.
jokes.setup: Argument about Charlie being un-American
jokes.punchline: Mac escalating to calling Charlie an enemy combatant over smoking
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of Mac's absurd patriotism

jokes.text: We're gonna go America all over their asses!
jokes.setup: Charlie getting fired up about proving his patriotism
jokes.punchline: Using 'America' as a verb for aggressive action
jokes.explanation: Memorable phrase that captures Charlie's manic patriotism perfectly

jokes.text: Oh, dude. This is just an acting gig. Like, I'm not really part of any cause.
jokes.setup: Charlie enthusiastically trying to join the anti-smoking cause
jokes.punchline: Actor reveals it's all fake, destroying Charlie's earnestness
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Charlie's misplaced enthusiasm

jokes.text: Oh, right. Like you know what you're talking about. How old are you? Like, 40?
jokes.setup: Dee dismissing the street actor
jokes.punchline: Actor hitting Dee with age insult
jokes.explanation: Standard age joke but works for Dee's vanity

jokes.text: Before I go America all over your ass!
jokes.setup: Charlie threatening the street actor
jokes.punchline: Callback to his earlier 'America all over' phrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Charlie's catchphrase

jokes.text: New Orleans was washed away in that terrible storm.
jokes.setup: Mac talking about wild places like New Orleans
jokes.punchline: Dennis matter-of-factly noting Hurricane Katrina destroyed it
jokes.explanation: Dark but understated reference to real tragedy

jokes.text: We want real girls going wild. It's important to see the transition. You want to watch the process.
jokes.setup: Mac suggesting they go to sororities
jokes.punchline: Dennis revealing his creepy psychological interest in corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - clinical and creepy about watching women's innocence disappear

jokes.text: Well, maybe for a small, fat person. Not for an actor.
jokes.setup: Charlie claiming his body is in tip-top shape
jokes.punchline: Dee's backhanded assessment of his physique
jokes.explanation: Standard body joke but fits Dee's condescending attitude

jokes.text: It blocks the babies out.
jokes.setup: Dee asking what Charlie knows about diaphragms
jokes.punchline: Charlie's childish understanding of birth control
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie innocence - reduces birth control to blocking babies

jokes.text: Locking up and maybe throwing up and stuff.
jokes.setup: Dee warning Charlie about stage fright
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly dramatic predictions of his failure
jokes.explanation: Weak escalation but shows Dee's pessimism

jokes.text: You girls have gone wild! You've gone wild!
jokes.setup: Girls Gone Wild-style bar environment
jokes.punchline: Overeager commentary on basic behavior
jokes.explanation: Functional physical comedy but relies on obvious premise

jokes.text: Except for the levees.
jokes.setup: Mac saying New Orleans had their shit figured out
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out their flood protection failure
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Katrina reference

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Segregation! That's segregation!
jokes.setup: Dennis suggesting keeping gamblers in the corner
jokes.punchline: Mac dramatically equating crowd control with civil rights violations
jokes.explanation: Good Mac overreaction to simple crowd control

jokes.text: Sounds like it's coming right out of Stalin's mouth.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to set boundaries
jokes.punchline: Extreme historical comparison to reasonable request
jokes.explanation: Good Frank escalation to historical extremes over minor bar management

jokes.text: I don't know who Stalin is. Who the hell is Stalin?
jokes.setup: Mac comparing something to Stalin
jokes.punchline: Frank's complete ignorance of major historical figure
jokes.explanation: Good Frank ignorance joke - wealthy but completely uneducated

jokes.text: Alan is just about to bet his wife's glass eye in the next hand.
jokes.setup: High-stakes gambling going on
jokes.punchline: Revealing the absurdly personal nature of the bets
jokes.explanation: Solid dark comedy - betting a spouse's prosthetic is perfectly twisted

jokes.text: Goddamn it, Charlie!
jokes.setup: Charlie attempting his dramatic tobacco performance
jokes.punchline: Dee's frustrated reaction to his inevitable failure
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - works but not special

jokes.text: You give her a free T-shirt and videotape it, and the clothes come right off.
jokes.setup: Comparing street harassment to their T-shirt scheme
jokes.punchline: Mac's crude analysis of how to manipulate women
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Mac's exploitative thinking

jokes.text: Can we get a couple glasses of milk?
jokes.setup: McPoyles entering the 'anything goes' bar
jokes.punchline: Their first request is bizarrely innocent milk
jokes.explanation: Perfect McPoyle weirdness - menacing presence ordering children's drinks

jokes.text: They bang each other, Dennis. And they bang each other.
jokes.setup: Discussing the McPoyles' disturbing behavior
jokes.punchline: Mac's repetitive, uncomfortable emphasis on their incest
jokes.explanation: Mac's disgusted repetition works for the cringe factor

jokes.text: It's a free country, bitch.
jokes.setup: Being told to keep their weird behavior out of the bar
jokes.punchline: McPoyle using freedom rhetoric to defend incest
jokes.explanation: Great perversion of American freedom rhetoric for sexual deviance

jokes.text: If Alan loses this hand, he's gonna start chopping off his fingers.
jokes.setup: High-stakes gambling in the basement
jokes.punchline: Escalation from betting possessions to self-mutilation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the gambling stakes to body parts

jokes.text: Looks like Jonestown.
jokes.setup: Charlie returning to see the destroyed bar
jokes.punchline: Comparing their failed experiment to mass suicide cult
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comparison - succinct and accurate assessment

jokes.text: Bump it.
jokes.setup: McPoyle trying to be casual with terrified Charlie
jokes.punchline: Menacing creep attempting normal social gesture
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - McPoyle's failed attempt at normal interaction

jokes.text: Don't tread on me.
jokes.setup: Charlie being upset about the McPoyles and lack of support
jokes.punchline: Using Revolutionary War motto for petty bar disputes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Charlie's misplaced patriotism

jokes.text: I am gonna act the shit out of somebody's ass.
jokes.setup: Dee being called a failure and getting defensive
jokes.punchline: Her garbled tough-guy threat about acting ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee - trying to sound tough but mangling the threat

jokes.text: Sweet, Sweet Dee... it's not something you can lose, okay?
jokes.setup: Discussion about still having acting talent
jokes.punchline: Artemis implying Dee never had any talent to begin with
jokes.explanation: Good subtle burn from Artemis about Dee's lack of talent

jokes.text: She wants to show you her boobies.
jokes.setup: McPoyle saying his mute sister wants to ask something
jokes.punchline: The 'question' is an incestuous proposition
jokes.explanation: Perfect McPoyle creepiness - proud of sister's body

jokes.text: There's nothing wrong with us. We're just expressing ourselves. Society has the problem.
jokes.setup: Being called out for their weird behavior
jokes.punchline: McPoyle using progressive rhetoric to defend incest
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of self-acceptance rhetoric for sexual deviance

jokes.text: The year is 1888. There's a killer on the loose. His name, Jack the Ripper.
jokes.setup: Street performance beginning
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic Jack the Ripper setup
jokes.explanation: Sets up for performance failure, but fairly standard pretentious theater opening

jokes.text: Get out of here! Get out of here! If you come to this bar, you're gonna get cancer and die! Attica! Attica!
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to do street performance
jokes.punchline: Abandoning the script to threaten customers with death
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie meltdown with random movie references

jokes.text: We gave people too much freedom. That's the problem! All they do is exploit it!
jokes.setup: The bar experiment descending into chaos
jokes.punchline: Dennis realizing the flaw in absolute freedom
jokes.explanation: Good thematic joke about libertarian ideals meeting reality

jokes.text: I'm sorry I sent you to jail, man. But any time you wanna stab me, it'd be really great for me.
jokes.setup: Charlie's defeated resignation
jokes.punchline: Actually inviting his enemies to stab him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie despair - so defeated he welcomes violence

jokes.text: Fork stabbed!
jokes.setup: McPoyle stabbing Charlie
jokes.punchline: Using a fork instead of a real weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd violence - stabbing with cutlery instead of knife

jokes.text: Nobody messes with the McPoyles!
jokes.setup: McPoyles being kicked out after fork stabbing
jokes.punchline: Declaring victory after attacking with dining utensil
jokes.explanation: Good McPoyle exit line but not exceptional

jokes.text: Whose fault is that, Captain America?
jokes.setup: Charlie complaining about being fork stabbed
jokes.punchline: Dennis sarcastically blaming Charlie's patriotic rhetoric
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Charlie's earlier patriotic posturing
